releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Fixes ### PDF export - **Page top margin**: images no longer stick to the top of continuation pages. Fixed a conflict where non-zero `printToPDF` margins overrode the CSS `@page` top margin, zeroing it on every page after the first. (#59) - **Image spacing**: excessive vertical gaps between consecutive images fixed — Obsidian merges adjacent image embeds into one paragraph, and the spacing collapse now correctly handles multi-media paragraphs. (#59) - **Soft line breaks**: text lines without blank lines between them now render on separate lines again (a prior CSS rule hiding `
` was silently merging them). (#59) ### Attachment handling - **Assets folder location**: when exporting a folder as Markdown or HTML, the `assets` folder now lands under the target folder (matching PDF/Word), instead of appearing a second time at the export root. (#60) - **Cross-file deduplication**: attachments sharing a name across different notes are now correctly deduplicated across the whole export. The collector previously reset its state per file. (#62) ### Content consistency across formats - **Document title**: the title no longer duplicates the body's first heading. When a note starts with a `# Heading`, it is now used as the document title and removed from the body. (#61) - **Markdown image alt text**: images in Markdown exports now include alt text. Empty alt (`![](...)`) caused images to not render in Obsidian and many viewers. (#61) - **Word soft line breaks**: adjacent text lines in DOCX no longer become separate paragraphs with large spacing — they now stay in one paragraph separated by line breaks. (#61) - **Word source-path comments**: `` comments no longer appear as literal visible text in DOCX output. (#61) --- **Full changelog**: https://github.com/rogerdigital/document-exporter/compare/0.4.7...0.4.8

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Demo

![Document Exporter selected files to PDF](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rogerdigital/document-exporter/main/docs/images/document-exporter-selected-files-pdf.gif)

Document Exporter exports selected Obsidian notes, folders, or query results into shareable document outputs. This demo highlights choosing specific files and exporting them as PDF.

## What's Changed
* Run workflows on Node 24 by @rogerdigital in https://github.com/rogerdigital/document-exporter/pull/48
* Fix plugin review findings by @rogerdigital in https://github.com/rogerdigital/document-exporter/pull/49
* Release 0.4.5 by @rogerdigital in https://github.com/rogerdigital/document-exporter/pull/50


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/rogerdigital/document-exporter/compare/0.4.4...0.4.5

releases.author: rogerdigital
releases.description:
## New features

- **Per-file export with directory structure preservation** — batch exports now create individual files maintaining the original folder hierarchy instead of combining into a single document
- **Batch export uses folder name** — the output folder name field replaces the file name field for folder and multi-file exports
- **Visual progress bar with cancel support** — a progress indicator appears during every export showing the current step or file; click Cancel to stop and keep already-exported files

## Improvements

- **Aligned source fields in export modal** — source type fields are now consistently positioned

## Breaking changes

- **Removed sort feature** — the sort mode setting and related UI have been removed from the export pipeline

releases.author: rogerdigital
releases.description:
Initial release of Document Exporter.

## Features
- Export notes, folders, and query results into Markdown bundles, HTML documents, and print-ready exports
- Multiple source types: current file, folder, selected files, filter by tag
- Sort by file path, file name, or frontmatter field
- Configurable output folder (vault-relative or system path)
